name: Tempura
recipeIngredient: 1 cup green beans, cut into 2-inch pieces
1 bulb of green onions, cut into 2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Make several crosswise slits on the underside of the shrimp to prevent curling.
2. Parboil the green beans until tender.
3. Pat the shrimp and vegetables dry with paper towels.
4. Arrange the dried shrimp and vegetables on a platter and cover; refrigerate until serving time.
5. Prepare the tempura batter and sauce recipes according to their respective instructions. Do not prepare the batter too far in advance.
6. Heat vegetable oil in a wok or deep skillet to 360 degrees Fahrenheit (175 degrees Celsius).
7. Dip the shrimp and vegetables into the prepared batter using tongs, allowing excess batter to drip back into the bowl.
8. Fry the battered shrimp and vegetables in batches until golden brown, turning once. Cooking time is approximately 2-3 minutes per batch.
9. Serve hot with tempura sauce on the side. Adjust the amount of batter mix as needed based on the quantities of seafood and vegetables used.
10. This recipe is best prepared close to or at serving time for optimal texture and flavor. It works well as a "tempura party" where guests can cook their own portions. (Does not keep well once cooked.)
